chr_mammography_screening.short_description: Percentage of female Medicare enrollees ages 65-74 receiving annual mammography screening.

chr_not_proficient_in_english.short_description: Percentage of population aged 5+ who reported speaking English less than well.

chr_children_eligible_for_free_or_reduce.long_name: Children Eligible for Free or Reduced Price Lunch

chr_driving_alone_to_work.long_name: Workers Who Drive Alone to Work

chr_some_college.short_description: Percentage of adults ages 25-44 with some post-secondary education.

chr_native_hawaiian_or_other_pacific_isl.long_name: Percentage Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

chr_uninsured_children.short_description: Percentage of children under age 19 without health insurance.
chr_uninsured_children.long_description: Percentage of children under age 19 without health insurance, from the Small Area Health Insurance Estimates (SAHIE) program.

chr_drinking_water_violations.short_description: Indicator of the presence of health-related drinking water violations.

chr_firearm_fatalities.short_description: Number of deaths due to firearms per 100,000 population.

chr.name: County Health Rankings & Roadmaps
chr.organization: University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute
chr.location: County Health Rankings & Roadmaps Annual Data
chr.description: The County Health Rankings & Roadmaps (CHR&R) program, a collaboration between the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation and the University of Wisconsin Population Health Institute, ranks the health of nearly all counties in the nation. The annual rankings measure vital health factors including high school graduation rates, obesity, smoking, unemployment, access to healthy foods, the quality of air and water, income inequality, and teen births that influence how long and how well we live. Data are released annually and cover all U.S. counties. Raw data are publicly available through the Zenodo repository (https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18157681) and span release years 2010 through the present.
